steps.title: Follow the sequence
steps.content: The sentence begins with "first consider," so the second clause should introduce the next action in a sequence: compare the first painting to another one.

steps.title: Use the next-step transition
steps.content: Choice A suggests substitution, choice C suggests continuation despite a contrast, and choice D suggests a result. None of those relationships fits a two-step procedure. The transition "next" correctly marks the second action after "first consider." The answer is B.
